steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to explain what 'plosive' means using the notes. The answer must define the term itself, not describe a single phoneme like /p/ in isolation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — describes /p/ as an example without defining plosive: Focuses on one specific phoneme rather than explaining the class of sounds the term refers to. ✗
  • Choice B — defines fricative instead: Describes sounds produced with continuous airflow friction, which is a different phoneme category entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — gives details about place of articulation for /p/: Still about the specific phoneme, not the defining feature of plosives as a group. ✗
  • Choice D — defines plosive as a sound in which airflow is at first fully blocked': Directly defines the term using the key feature from the notes — complete airflow stoppage followed by release. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
